title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument includes Node

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument includes Node
input_row.description: Restrict kubelet nodes to reading only objects associated with them.
input_row.rationale: The Node authorization mode only allows kubelets to read Secret, ConfigMap, PersistentVolume, and PersistentVolumeClaim objects associated with their nodes. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --authorization-mode argument exists and is set to a value to include Node.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --authorization-mode parameter to a value that includes Node. --authorization-mode=Node,RBAC Default Value: By default, Node authorization is not enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authorization/node/ 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/46076 4. https://acotten.com/post/kube17-security

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_authorization_mode_node_included

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver Pod specification, typically located in the kube-system namespace as a static Pod (manifest at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml, but also visible via the Kubernetes API as a Pod).
- Use the Kubernetes API or kubectl to retrieve the kube-apiserver Pod spec:
  - `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o json`
  - Or, for static pods, inspect the manifest directly if accessible via automation.
- Parse the command/args field of the kube-apiserver container to extract the value(s) of the `--authorization-mode` flag.
- Validate that the `--authorization-mode` flag exists and that its value includes `Node` (case-sensitive, comma-separated list).
- **Pass Criteria:** The `--authorization-mode` flag is present and includes `Node` (e.g., `--authorization-mode=Node,RBAC`).
- **Fail Criteria:** The `--authorization-mode` flag is missing or does not include `Node`.
- This check can be fully automated using Kubernetes API access and does not require host-level or subjective review.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11 PDF
